https://liberty-note.com/2024/12/05/sv-d.summary: **VGC FORMAT: VGC Regulation H** **TITLE: [S24 Double Final 19th Place] Frosmoth Snow Team** **Pokémon 1: Frosmoth** - Ability: Ice Scales - Held Item: Leftovers - Tera Type: Water - Nature: Bold - Moves: Blizzard / Wide Guard / Quiver Dance / Tera Blast - EV Spread: 252 0 236 4 4 12 - EV Explanation: HP: 16n+1, Defence: 11n. The main attacker of this team. The HP and Defence EVs allow it to withstand physical attacks to some extent, and it raises its Special Attack, Special Defence, and Speed with Quiver Dance to attack. It has high special bulk and often stays on the field, so Leftovers was chosen over Sitrus Berry. Blizzard was chosen as a high-power, albeit inaccurate, spread move. Wide Guard is used to support the team. Tera Type Water was chosen because it has good offensive and defensive synergy and is effective against the prevalent rain teams. **Pokémon 2: Volcarona** - Ability: Flame Body - Held Item: Rocky Helmet - Tera Type: Steel - Nature: Bold - Moves: Heat Wave / Will-O-Wisp / Rage Powder / Bug Buzz - EV Spread: 252 0 236 4 4 12 - EV Explanation: HP: 16n. A support role. By increasing physical bulk and using Rage Powder, Flame Body and Rocky Helmet are used to inflict burns and damage. Bug Buzz is used to support against special attackers. Tera Type Steel was chosen because the team is weak to Rock, and Steel has many resistances. **Pokémon 3: Alolan Ninetales** - Ability: Snow Warning - Held Item: Life Orb - Tera Type: Ghost - Nature: Timid - Moves: Freeze-Dry / Aurora Veil / Icy Wind / Moonblast - EV Spread: 4 0 20 252 4 228 - EV Explanation: HP: 10n-1, Speed: Outspeeds base 105s. Used as a weather setter and fast attacker. Icy Wind is used for Speed control, and it can also use Fairy moves, which are lacking in Regulation H. Freeze-Dry was chosen for its good coverage. Life Orb was chosen because the recoil damage can be used to switch out and preserve Aurora Veil turns, but there were not many opportunities to do so. Tera Type Ghost was chosen because it resists Poison, which is a weakness, and it can negate Fake Out. **Pokémon 4: Garchomp** - Ability: Rough Skin - Held Item: Clear Amulet - Tera Type: Fire - Nature: Jolly - Moves: Stomping Tantrum / Protect / Rock Slide / Wide Guard - EV Spread: 0 252 0 0 4 252 - EV Explanation: Speed: Maximum Speed, to be faster than other Garchomp. It was chosen because it complements Frosmoth's offensive and defensive type matchups. Its high stats and Wide Guard are useful against physical attackers that Frosmoth struggles with, and Rough Skin can chip away at HP. Tera Type Fire was chosen because it is strong against the prevalent Incineroar and Rillaboom, and it prevents burns. **Pokémon 5: Incineroar** - Ability: Intimidate - Held Item: Sitrus Berry - Tera Type: Ghost - Nature: Careful - Moves: Flare Blitz / Parting Shot / Fake Out / Knock Off - EV Spread: 252 4 0 0 252 0 - EV Explanation: It can support with Intimidate and Fake Out, and it was chosen to overcome the team's weakness to stall. It is designed with HP and Special Defence in mind, considering that it will be used against Ursaluna (Bloodmoon) and Flutter Mane. Knock Off is a strong move that can remove the opponent's information. **Pokémon 6: Rillaboom** - Ability: Grassy Surge - Held Item: Assault Vest - Tera Type: Fire - Nature: Adamant - Moves: Grassy Glide / Wood Hammer / High Horsepower / Fake Out - EV Spread: 252 252 0 0 0 4 - EV Explanation: It was chosen because it is advantageous against Water types that Frosmoth cannot hit for super-effective damage, and against sand teams with many physical attackers. Wood Hammer and Grassy Glide are strong in Grassy Terrain. **VGC FORMAT ANALYSIS:** VGC Regulation H is a format within the Scarlet and Violet series that allows the use of Paradox Pokémon and certain restricted Pokémon. The format is defined by the Terastallization mechanic, which allows Pokémon to change their type, influencing both offensive and defensive strategies. Common meta trends include strong restricted Pokémon like Calyrex-Shadow Rider and Miraidon, as well as powerful Paradox Pokémon like Flutter Mane and Iron Hands. This team's viability depends on its ability to control the weather with Alolan Ninetales and leverage the snow to boost Frosmoth's Blizzard. The team also aims to disrupt opponents with Incineroar's Intimidate and Fake Out, and Rillaboom's Grassy Surge. **CONCLUSION:** This team is built around Frosmoth, using Alolan Ninetales to set up snow and boost its Blizzard. The team aims to control the pace of the battle, disrupt opponents with Incineroar and Rillaboom, and capitalize on favorable matchups. The team's success depends on its ability to manage weather conditions and leverage the Terastallization mechanic effectively. **FINAL ARTICLE SUMMARY:** The article details a VGC Regulation H team built around Frosmoth, aiming to leverage snow to boost its Blizzard. The team includes Alolan Ninetales for weather control, Volcarona for support, Garchomp for type coverage and disruption, Incineroar for Intimidate and Fake Out, and Rillaboom for Grassy Terrain and offensive pressure. **TEAM STRENGTHS:** - Good synergy between Frosmoth and Alolan Ninetales in snow conditions. - Wide Guard support from Frosmoth and Garchomp. - Incineroar's Intimidate and Fake Out provide disruption and control. - Rillaboom's Grassy Terrain provides healing and offensive pressure. **TEAM WEAKNESSES:** - Weakness to Fighting-types like Annihilape. - Difficulty dealing with setup sweepers like Ursaluna (Bloodmoon) in Trick Room. - Susceptible to strategies that remove or disrupt weather conditions. **AUTHOR'S ASSESSMENT:** The author notes that the team relies on controlling the pace of the battle and capitalizing on favorable matchups. The team struggles against certain setup sweepers and strategies that disrupt weather conditions. The author also mentions that the team requires patient play, as it often aims to outlast opponents and win in the mid-to-late game.
